stem: In a right triangle, the angle of measure x degrees is acute. The side opposite this angle has length 24 units, and the side adjacent to this angle (other than the hypotenuse) has length 7 units. What is the value of the tangent of x degrees?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The tangent of an acute angle in a right triangle equals the ratio of the length of the side opposite the angle to the length of the side adjacent to the angle: 24 over 7.

Choice B is incorrect: this is cotangent.

Choice C is incorrect: this is sine.

Choice D is incorrect: this is cosine.
